text_blocks.text: AFL Global and Ganlan Media were
created in 2012 by businessman Martin E. Judge,
founder and owner of the Judge Group. The
company, called AFL Global, LLC, looks to
introduce and launch professional Arena
Football teams and franchises in various locations
throughout the world (like NFL Europe). After
their successful trip to China to help promote
the game, they formally announced plans to
further develop AFL China by the fall of 2014
by starting a comprehensive training program in
May 2013 with exhibition games planned for
the cities of Beijing and Guangzhou in October,
This is the first time professional football of
any kind will be played in China with the
support of the Chinese government and the
CRFA (Chinese Rugby Football Association). Key
persons involved include founder and CEO.
Martin E. Judge, partner Ron Jaworski, CAFL
CEO Gary Morris and president David Niu.
Ganlan Media has since dropped this name
and will carry the league's name as its corporate
identity.
